question: Does training xlmr-560m on a REAL legal-STRUCTURE corpus (CJEU real-skeleton: mined CJEU layout signatures + authored connective prose + checksum-valid synthetic PII) transfer to the TAB real-legal board ZERO-SHOT better than generic synthetic (which caps ~0.30)? Decisive bounded gate for whether to scale the multilingual RES-72 corpus.

eval.name: TAB ECHR legal English (REAL peer-reviewed gold)
eval.scoring: entity_f1 via scorecard_klu106 (_predict/_mask/_entity_f1_on_rows) — EXACT RES-97 path; the SAME path as every board number. Deterministic CPU scoring (EUROPRIV_DEVICE=cpu).

generic_synthetic_xlmr560m_ceiling.note: RES-97 reports xlmr-560m TAB across seeds 0.244 / 0.368 (mean 0.306); RES-104.2a matched 2k arms 0.296 (stage-A) / 0.230 (stage-B). The generic-synthetic regime caps ~0.24-0.30.

presidio_target.note: Rule/model hybrid reference target on the same TAB board.

contamination_assert.claim: No TAB test doc text appears in the CJEU training corpus.
contamination_assert.rationale: TAB is ECHR/HUDOC (Council of Europe); the corpus is CJEU-structure (different court/collection) + synthetic PII, so there is no document overlap by construction.
contamination_assert.result: PASS — 0 full-doc and 0 sentence-level verbatim overlaps.

decision.recommendation: Scale to the full multilingual RES-72 build. The CJEU real-structure corpus clears the generic-synthetic ceiling on real-legal zero-shot transfer (TAB-F1 0.3399 vs generic 40k 0.2439 and ceiling ~0.30). Real legal STRUCTURE is a working lever; scale-up is justified.
decision.caveat: Single seed -> directional, not a CI-backed claim. Real-structure / synthetic-PII corpus (NOT real PII). The 20k corpus is larger than the 2k RES-104.2a arms and smaller than the 40k generic board entry; absolute numbers are indicative of the regime, not a strict matched-size comparison.

guards.config_status: dev (synthetic training; TAB is real external gold)
